rationale_for_baseline_tier: Suspected obstructing colorectal malignancy with an acute partial/impending large bowel obstruction: crescendo colicky pain, distension, altered stool calibre, melaena and marked weight loss. This is a surgical emergency-in-evolution requiring same-day Emergency Department assessment (surgical review, IV hydration, bloods, urgent CT). The patient is currently haemodynamically stable (no shock, no peritonism, no faeculent vomiting) and has family transport available, so the correct baseline is T4 (ED, hours, family transport) rather than T5. The T4/T5 ceiling is respected: this exceeds a routine referral because patient-reportable time-critical features (obstruction with melaena, escalating pain) are present, but a 000 ambulance is reserved for emergent deterioration. A T5 000-edge is armed for perforation / complete obstruction / haemodynamic compromise. Down-triaging below T3 (routine GP, watchful waiting, laxative advice) would be a critical under-triage.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Vomiting stool-like (faeculent) material, sudden change from wave-like cramps to constant tearing/unbearable pain, a hard rigid abdomen, or high fever/rigors
escalation_edges.rationale: These indicate complete obstruction or perforation with peritonitis — a life-threatening surgical emergency needing ambulance transfer and emergency surgery.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you start vomiting material that looks or smells like stool, the pain suddenly becomes one constant tearing agony instead of coming in waves, your belly goes hard and rigid, or you spike a fever with shakes — call 000 for an ambulance immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Large-volume fresh rectal bleeding, passing a lot of blood/clots, or feeling faint, dizzy, clammy or collapsing
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals significant GI haemorrhage / haemodynamic compromise on a background of anaemia — needs emergency resuscitation and transfusion.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you pass a large amount of blood or clots, or feel faint, dizzy, sweaty or like you might pass out, call 000 straight away — do not wait.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Baseline presentation — obstructive colicky pain with alarm features but currently stable
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — go to the Emergency Department now (family transport)
escalation_edges.rationale: The suspected obstruction and unexcludable malignancy mandate urgent same-day hospital assessment and CT even without perforation signs; delaying to routine hours risks progression to complete/closed-loop obstruction.
escalation_edges.communication_template: You need to go to the Emergency Department today — have your family take you now. Tell them you have severe cramping, a blocked-up belly and have been passing dark blood, and that you have not eaten. They will do blood tests and a scan.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Older adult with cachexia and probable significant anaemia
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Raises malignancy probability and lowers physiological reserve — reinforces urgent same-day assessment and a low threshold for 000 if any deterioration; frailty means shock can develop quickly.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Relies on adult offspring for transport; family present and able to drive
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Family transport makes prompt ED self-presentation (T4) feasible; if transport were unavailable or the patient deteriorated, escalate to 000 (T5) rather than delay.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need urgent hospital assessment today for a possible blockage in your bowel. Have your family take you to the Emergency Department now. Do not eat any solid food — take only small sips of clear fluid — and do NOT take any laxatives. Bring a list of your medications. If you cannot get transport quickly, or if you get worse on the way, call 000 for an ambulance.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Go to the Emergency Department today (family transport); call 000 if deterioration or no transport
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: This cannot wait for routine clinic hours — the Emergency Department is open 24 hours and is the right place tonight. Call 000 at any hour if the emergency triggers below appear.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth encounter identified alarm features of a suspected obstructing lower-GI malignancy (change in bowel habit, melaena/rectal bleeding, ~10 kg weight loss, escalating obstructive colicky pain) with a suspected partial/impending large bowel obstruction; escalated for same-day ED assessment and urgent CT, and flagged for the lower-GI 2-week-wait suspected-cancer pathway. Nil by mouth advised; laxatives explicitly withheld.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as constipation/haemorrhoids/IBS with routine or self-care advice, or recommending a laxative — missing both the malignancy alarm features and the acute obstruction.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Progression to complete/closed-loop obstruction, ischaemia and perforation with faecal peritonitis and death; and delayed cancer diagnosis.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising urgency but arranging only same-day GP/urgent-care review without ensuring surgical assessment and CT, under-appreciating the obstruction (acceptable only if that pathway guarantees same-day surgical assessment and imaging).

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling a 000 ambulance for a currently stable patient with available family transport — safe and defensible (never penalise on the safe side for a possible obstruction), but a notch above the resource-optimal T4.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the obstruction is partial or progressing to complete, and the true severity of anaemia/blood loss — none quantifiable via telehealth (no exam, bloods or imaging).
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as a time-critical evolving surgical problem: arrange same-day ED assessment and CT, give conservative nil-by-mouth/no-laxative advice, and arm explicit 000 triggers for deterioration.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using the absence of peritonism/shock or the currently stable vitals to reassure and defer to a routine appointment, or attempting to confirm/exclude cancer or obstruction remotely.
